matter.title: Marcus and Elena Ortega fake Chapter 13 intake
matter.clientGoals: Stop foreclosure, cure mortgage arrears, keep both vehicles, and repay priority taxes through plan.
matter.urgentConcerns: Mortgage sale date notice says sale is scheduled within 30 days.
matter.priorBankruptcyDetails: Marcus filed Chapter 7 in Utah around 2017 and received a discharge. Elena has not filed before.

assets.keepIntent: Keep and cure arrears through Chapter 13 plan

assets.description: Household furniture, appliances, clothing, electronics, and school laptops

debts.notes: Foreclosure sale date noticed. Need arrears cure treatment.
Two payments behind; wants to keep.
Estimated priority portion from recent tax years; verify returns and transcripts.
Multiple cards consolidated as client estimate.

sofaEvents.description: Foreclosure sale notice from Beehive Mortgage Servicing.
Recent federal income tax balance disclosed by debtors.

chapter13.disposableIncomeNotes: Fake data suggests rough net monthly income of $10,520 less expenses of about $7,850 before plan payment. Verify payroll deductions, taxes, and allowed expenses.

matter.title: Theo Bennett fake self-employed Chapter 7 intake
matter.clientGoals: Discharge business-related unsecured debt, understand tax debt treatment, keep work van if possible.
matter.urgentConcerns: Supplier lawsuit and possible levy against business checking account.

assets.description: Mobile repair tools, compressor, diagnostic tablet, inventory bins
assets.exemptionNote: Attorney must review tool-of-trade and business asset exemptions.

assets.description: Customer receivables for completed repair work
Apartment furnishings, clothing, phone, basic electronics

debts.notes: Client says taxes are from 2023 and 2024. Need return dates/transcripts.
Secured work vehicle debt, two payments behind.
Supplier lawsuit filed; client also paid $2,000 to brother for a business loan.

income.employer: Bennett Mobile Repair LLC / sole proprietor

expenses.notes: Fuel and maintenance for work van

sofaEvents.description: Supplier collection action filed by Mile High Parts Supply.
Payment to brother for old business loan within one year.
Tax balance disclosed for recent self-employment years.

chapter7.securedPropertyIntent: Keep work van if secured debt and exemption review permit.
chapter7.assetDistributionEstimate: Business tools, receivables, and vehicle equity require exemption and trustee-risk review.
